name: Tsechu
traditions: Masked cham dances; unfurling giant thongdrel (silk appliqué); traditional music; archery; community feasting.
historicalBackground: Tsechus are annual Buddhist festivals held in every district of Bhutan, dating to the 17th century under Zhabdrung Ngawang Namgyal.
description: Bhutan's spectacular masked dance festivals, where monks perform sacred cham dances in colorful monasteries across the Himalayan kingdom.
